Title: GmMYB4 Positively Regulates Isoflavone Biosynthesis via the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW Module in Soybean

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: NJAU_32; assays in N. benthamiana for transient systems
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As part of MBW complex, contributes to repression of GmIFS2 and activation of GmANS3, thereby favoring anthocyanin over isoflavone branch output.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to rebalance flux between anthocyanin and isoflavone pathways, though not the main engineering focus of this paper.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB115 forms an MBW complex with GmTT8 and GmTTG1. 2. Together with GmTT8, it binds promoters of key phenylpropanoid/isoflavone/anthocyanin genes. 3. This complex suppresses GmIFS2 promoter activity and activates GmANS3 promoter activity. 4. GmMYB4 disrupts the GmMYB115-GmTT8 interaction.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although mechanistically important, GmMYB115 was not genetically manipulated here and serves mainly as a complex component mediating GmMYB4 effects.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as bHLH partner in MBW complex that represses isoflavone branch gene GmIFS2 and activates anthocyanin gene GmANS3; target of competitive interference by GmMYB4.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point for redirecting phenylpropanoid flux between anthocyanin and isoflavone pathways.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmTT8 interacts with GmMYB4 and GmMYB115. 2. In complex with GmMYB115, it enhances binding to promoters of key pathway genes. 3. The MBW complex containing GmTT8 suppresses GmIFS2 and activates GmANS3. 4. GmMYB4 competes for GmTT8 binding and disrupts this complex.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as the principal bHLH component for mechanistic dissection of MBW complex action in this study.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Component of the MBW complex used to regulate promoter activity of competing isoflavone and anthocyanin genes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC context based on MBW complex reconstitution; literature-supported complex membership cited in paper
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary target for MBW engineering, though direct evidence in this paper is limited.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmTTG1 was included as a component of the GmMYB115-GmTT8-GmTTG1 MBW complex. 2. The reconstituted MBW complex suppresses GmIFS2 promoter activity and activates GmANS3 promoter activity. 3. GmMYB4 disrupts MBW function upstream of these promoter effects.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its direct binding role was not independently dissected here; included because the paper defines the operative complex as GmMYB115-GmTT8-GmTTG1.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Competes with isoflavone biosynthesis; expression is reduced by GmMYB4 and GmMAPK6, consistent with lowered anthocyanin content.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in GmMYB4 overexpression lines and GmMAPK6-overexpressing hairy roots; upregulated in gmmyb4 mutants
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; Y1H with MBW components; Dual-LUC; anthocyanin metabolite quantification
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential branch-competition target for redirecting carbon flux toward isoflavones.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmANS3 is a competing anthocyanin-pathway gene showing the opposite expression trend to isoflavone genes. 2. The MBW complex activates GmANS3 promoter activity. 3. GmMYB4 suppresses this activation by disrupting MBW complex formation. 4. Lower GmANS3 expression is associated with reduced anthocyanin and enhanced isoflavone accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a common gene because its importance in this study is branch competition rather than the paper's main focal gene discovery.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased isoflavone content in hairy roots, but downstream gene-expression effects were more limited than for GmMAPK6.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC; Co-IP; Y2H with constitutively active mutant; hairy-root overexpression; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Hairy-root overexpression construct promoter not explicitly stated
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible upstream signaling target, but less strongly supported than GmMAPK6 for engineering isoflavone traits.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMAPKK2 interacts with GmMYB4. 2. Overexpression in hairy roots significantly increased isoflavone content. 3. Its transcriptional effects on pathway genes were narrower than those of GmMAPK6.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper suggests involvement in the signaling module, but mechanistic placement relative to GmMAPK6 was not fully resolved.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes entry into the isoflavone branch from the general flavonoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme supported by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in GmMYB4 backgrounds; overexpression in soybean with isoflavone quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in GmMYB4 overexpression lines and GmMAPK6-overexpressing hairy roots; downregulated in gmmyb4 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmIFS2 expression is positively regulated by GmMYB4. 2. GmIFS2 overexpression significantly increased total isoflavone content in leaves and seeds. 3. The MBW complex suppresses GmIFS2 promoter activity, and GmMYB4 alleviates this suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme target for increasing soybean isoflavone content; may be combined with GmMYB4/GmMAPK6 manipulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its promoter is indirectly regulated through the GmMYB115-GmTT8-GmTTG1 MBW module rather than direct binding by GmMYB4.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family; contains bHLH TF-binding motif in R3 domain and EAR motif
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Isoflavone biosynthesis; anthocyanin branch selection; broader flavonoid and proanthocyanidin metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds important for plant function and human health
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates isoflavone accumulation and shifts flux away from anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator identified by GWAS and functional validation; central node of the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW module
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect transcriptional regulation via disruption of MBW complex assembly; competitive binding to bHLH proteins
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmIFS2; GmANS3; GmPAL1.3; GmC4H; GmCHS8; GmCHR1; GmCHI1; GmIFS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/is associated with increased expression of isoflavone pathway genes including GmIFS2 and represses/lowers GmANS3 expression through MBW disruption
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmTT8; GmGL3; GmEGL3; GmMAPK6; GmMAPKK2; GmMYB115
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with bHLH members of the MBW complex and disrupts the GmMYB115-GmTT8(-GmTTG1) complex
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GmMAPK6-mediated phosphorylation and positive transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, flowers, pods and seeds; highest in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed across multiple soybean tissues, peaking in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; haplotype analysis; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; soybean hairy-root overexpression and RNAi; stable overexpression lines; CRISPR knockout; RNA-seq; DAP-seq; EMSA; LCI; BiFC; Y2H; Dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using resequencing SNP data from 219 accessions; RNA-seq (Illumina NovaSeq 6000, accession NA); DAP-seq (Illumina NovaSeq 6000, accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Three field environments in Nanjing (2014, 2015, 2018); controlled growth chamber conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression decreased anthocyanin content in leaves while increasing isoflavones, indicating flux reallocation between competing branches
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) for transient assays/subcellular localization; pBA002 overexpression construct; hairy-root OE/RNAi constructs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favourable haplotype GmMYB4H1 and transgenic/editing strategies can be used to breed high-isoflavone soybean varieties.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS and haplotype analysis identified GmMYB4 as the candidate gene at qGlc11 associated with glycitein class content. 2. Overexpression increased TIC, GEC, DAC and GLC, whereas RNAi and knockout reduced them. 3. GmMYB4 interacts with GmTT8/GmGL3/GmEGL3 and disrupts the GmMYB115-GmTT8 MBW complex. 4. This disruption relieves repression of GmIFS2 and reduces activation of GmANS3, favoring isoflavone over anthocyanin accumulation. 5. GmMYB4 also promotes flavonoid and proanthocyanidin accumulation more broadly.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmMYB4 does not directly bind tested promoters of 10 pathway genes in Y1H, indicating mainly indirect regulation despite broad DAP-seq target enrichment in flavonoid pathways.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Isoflavone biosynthesis via upstream control of GmMYB4 and pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hairy roots; inferred leaves and seeds through pathway regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoflavone accumulation by increasing GmMYB4 expression and phosphorylating GmMYB4.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream positive signaling component that links MAPK cascade to isoflavone biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Phosphorylation and positive transcriptional activation of regulator/pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmMYB4; GmPAL1.3; Gm4CL; GmCHI1; GmCHR1; GmIFS2; GmIFS1; GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates GmMYB4 and multiple isoflavone biosynthetic genes; downregulates GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts physically with GmMYB4; part of MAPK signaling cascade context
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MAPK cascade context; potentially activated by GmMAPKK2 in pathway context, but not fully established here
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed in overexpressing hairy roots; caused induction of GmMYB4 and pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC; Co-IP; Y2H with constitutively active mutant; in vitro kinase assay plus LC-MS/MS; hairy-root overexpression and metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: PPI prediction using STRING; LC-MS/MS phosphosite identification
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter competing anthocyanin branch via downregulating GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Hairy-root overexpression vector promoter not explicitly stated; constitutive transient-expression systems used in interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising upstream engineering target to boost isoflavones through both signaling and transcriptional control; suitable for stacking with GmMYB4/IFS2.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMAPK6 physically interacts with GmMYB4. 2. Constitutively active GmMAPK6 phosphorylates GmMYB4 at Ser39 in vitro. 3. Overexpression of GmMAPK6 in soybean hairy roots increased isoflavone content. 4. GmMAPK6 upregulated GmMYB4 and multiple isoflavone pathway genes while downregulating GmANS3.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper proposes both transcriptional and post-translational control of GmMYB4 by GmMAPK6; in vivo phosphosite validation remains future work.
